# TechnicalAI — UX Improvement Spec

> **How to use this doc:** Each section below describes a specific UX improvement with reference to the current screenshot, followed by exact changes to implement. Copy the full document into a Claude Code conversation.

---

## Current screens (reference)

### Screen 1 — Top Picks / Screener cards

![Top Picks screen](data:image/png;base64,)

### Screen 2 — All Setups list

![All Setups screen](data:image/png;base64,)

### Screen 3 — Chart Analysis tab

![Chart Analysis screen](data:image/png;base64,)

### Screen 4 — Track Record tab

![Track Record screen](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## Improvement 1 — Screener cards: visual hierarchy + draft CTA

**Problem:** In the top picks and all setups views, the "Draft" and "Analyze Chart" buttons have equal visual weight. Users can accidentally skip adding to draft. The bullet-point signal list requires reading, not scanning.

**Changes:**

### 1a. Score badge — color-coded by value

Replace the current plain score number with a colored badge:

- Score ≥ 80 → green text (`#3B6D11`)
- Score 60–79 → amber text (`#854F0B`)
- Score < 60 → red text (`#A32D2D`)
- Font size: 22px, weight 500
- Place in top-right of card header

### 1b. Signal bullets → compact tag pills

Replace the 3–5 bullet points with small pill badges. Each pill is `background: #F1EFE8; color: #5F5E5A; font-size: 11px; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 4px`.

Map the current bullets to pills:

- "X% from breakout level" → pill: `0.1% to breakout`
- "Volume X× above 50d avg" → pill: `Vol 7.8×`
- "RSI XX — momentum zone" → pill colored green if RSI > 60: `RSI 74`
- "Breaking out now" → pill colored green: `Breaking out`

### 1c. Button hierarchy — primary vs secondary

- **"+ Add to draft"** → primary button: `background: #E6F1FB; color: #185FA5; border: none; flex: 1; font-weight: 500`
- **"Analyze Chart →"** → secondary button: `border: 0.5px solid #ccc; background: transparent`
- Stack them in a row: `[+ Add to draft (flex:1)] [Analyze →]`

### 1d. After adding to draft — button state change

When user clicks "+ Add to draft":

- Button changes to `✓ In draft` with `background: #EAF3DE; color: #3B6D11; border: none`
- Button becomes disabled (non-clickable)
- This prevents accidental double-adds

---

## Improvement 2 — Draft panel: queue UX

**Problem (see Chart Analysis screen):** The draft sidebar shows a flat list "2 ready · click to view". There's no visible action to move a stock to the watchlist from there, and no keyboard navigation hint. The user has to remember to go back and do it manually.

**Changes:**

### 2a. Header — rename + count badge

- Label: `Draft queue` (instead of just "Draft 2")
- Add amber badge: `background: #FAEEDA; color: #854F0B; font-size: 11px` showing `2 ready`

### 2b. Per-card colored left border

Each card in the draft sidebar gets a 2px left border:

- Green (`#639922`) if the stock came from the screener with a confirmed setup
- Amber (`#E9A319`) if manually added

### 2c. Per-card action icons

Each draft card shows two small icon buttons (20×20px, top right of card):

- ★ icon → `background: #E6F1FB; color: #185FA5; border-radius: 4px` — clicking adds to watchlist immediately
- × icon → `background: var(--bg-secondary); color: var(--text-secondary)` — removes from draft

On ★ click: remove from draft, add to watchlist, show a brief toast "EXAS added to watchlist"

### 2d. Keyboard navigation hint

Below the card list, add a single line in 10px muted text:
`← → to switch  ·  ★ to watchlist`

Implement ← → arrow keys to navigate between draft stocks in the chart view (load the prev/next stock automatically).

---

## Improvement 3 — Track Record: add expectancy + per-pattern breakdown

**Problem (see Track Record screen):** The current view shows Total Setups, Win Rate, Avg Return, Best Trade, Worst Trade, Active. Win rate alone (0W/2L) is demoralizing and incomplete — a trader with 30% win rate can be very profitable with good R:R. There's also no breakdown by pattern, so users can't learn which setups work for them.

**Changes:**

### 3a. Add Expectancy metric card

Insert a new metric card between Avg Return and Best Trade:

```
Expectancy = (Win Rate × Avg Win) − (Loss Rate × Avg Loss)
```

- Display as a % with sign: `+1.8%` in green or `−0.4%` in red
- Label: `Expectancy`
- Tooltip on hover: "Average expected return per trade, accounting for both win rate and size"

### 3b. Per-pattern breakdown section

Below the summary metric cards, add a new section "By pattern":

For each unique pattern in the user's track record, show a row:

```
[Pattern badge]  [progress bar showing win %]  [3W / 1L · +5.1% avg]
```

- Pattern badge colors match those used in the screener (purple for Inv H&S, blue for SMA Bounce, amber for Cup & Handle, etc.)
- Progress bar: green fill for win %, right-aligned text shows W/L count and avg return
- Sort by avg return descending (best pattern first)
- This lets users immediately see "Cup & Handle is underperforming for me"

### 3c. Live setup card — show R:R prominently

In the "Live Setups" card (currently shows TSLA with Entry/Stop/Target):

- Add `R/R 2.5:1` as a colored badge next to the confidence badge
- Color: green if R/R ≥ 2, amber if 1–2, red if < 1

---

## Improvement 4 — Persistent draft bar across all tabs

**Problem:** The draft is only visible inside the Chart Analysis sidebar. When the user is on Top Picks or Track Record, they have no reminder of what's in their draft, and no quick way to get back to reviewing it.

**Implementation:**

### 4a. Persistent bottom bar component

When the draft is non-empty, render a slim fixed bar at the bottom of every page/tab:

```
[amber badge: Draft · 2 stocks]  [EXAS, COP]  [Review in Chart Analysis →]
```

- Height: ~40px
- Background: `var(--color-background-primary)`
- Top border: `0.5px solid var(--color-border-tertiary)`
- The stock tickers listed in muted text (truncate after 3: "EXAS, COP +1 more")
- "Review in Chart Analysis →" is a primary-style link that navigates to the Chart Analysis tab with the first draft stock pre-loaded

### 4b. Collapse when empty

When draft is empty, the bar disappears entirely (no empty state needed — absence is sufficient).

### 4c. Quick-add from bar

The bar can also have a small `+ Add ticker` text input inline (optional, power-user feature) so users can add a ticker directly from any tab without navigating away.

---

## Improvement 5 — Flow: watchlist → track record transition

**Problem:** When a user moves a stock from draft to watchlist, it's not immediately clear that a trade setup has been created and is now being tracked. The Track Record tab appears separate.

**Changes:**

### 5a. Toast on watchlist add

When ★ is clicked on a draft card (or "Move to Watchlist" is clicked):
Show a toast notification for 3 seconds:

```
✓ EXAS added to watchlist — trade setup is now being tracked
              [View in Track Record →]
```

Toast style: dark background, white text, bottom-right corner.

### 5b. Track Record tab — badge on new entries

When a new stock enters the track record (status: "Waiting for entry"), show a blue dot badge on the "Track Record" nav tab until the user visits it. This draws attention to the fact that something new is there.

### 5c. "Waiting for entry" card — show current price vs entry

In the live setups card (currently shows Entry / Stop / Target), add a fourth data point:

- `Current price: $378.20`
- Color it relative to entry: green if below entry (good for longs waiting to pull back), red if above entry (missed the level)

---

## Summary of priority order

| Priority | Improvement                                  | Effort | Impact                        |
| -------- | -------------------------------------------- | ------ | ----------------------------- |
| 1        | Persistent draft bottom bar                  | Low    | High — fixes biggest flow gap |
| 2        | Draft panel: ★ to watchlist + keyboard nav   | Low    | High — removes manual step    |
| 3        | Screener cards: button hierarchy + state     | Low    | Medium — reduces mis-clicks   |
| 4        | Track record: expectancy + pattern breakdown | Medium | High — makes data educational |
| 5        | Screener cards: signal pills                 | Low    | Medium — faster scanning      |
| 6        | Toast + track record badge on watchlist add  | Low    | Medium — closes the loop      |
